commonIssues:
Difficulty in providing pre-independence or old family documents to verify caste lineage, especially in tribal and rural areas.

How to fix:

  • Obtain old school admission registers of parents/grandparents.
  • Request Khasra-Khatauni records from the Patwari — these may mention caste.
  • Submit a sworn affidavit from two community elders along with their own caste certificates.
  • Apply via MP e-District Portal.

MP requires Samagra ID for most government services. If the Samagra ID has incorrect caste or family details, the caste certificate application gets rejected.

How to fix:

  • Verify your Samagra ID details at Samagra Portal before applying.
  • If caste is incorrect in Samagra, apply for Samagra profile correction at the nearest Gram Panchayat/Ward office.
  • Carry Aadhaar, school records, and family certificates for correction.
  • Call the Samagra Helpline 0755-2700800 for support.

Applications pending at Tehsildar or SDM level for months due to backlog or inaction.

How to fix:

  • Track status on MP e-District Portal.
  • File a grievance at CM Helpline / Samadhan Portal if delayed beyond 15 days.
  • Call the CM Helpline 181 for immediate escalation.
  • Invoke the MP Public Services Guarantee Act for time-bound delivery.

Tribal communities in areas like Jhabua, Dhar, Mandla, and Balaghat face additional scrutiny and extended verification for ST certificates.

How to fix:

  • Submit Tribal Welfare Department records or forest rights certificates as additional proof.
  • Provide community certificates of relatives already verified by the government.
  • Contact the Tribal Welfare Commissioner office for assistance.
  • Seek help from NALSA for free legal aid.

Server errors, OTP failures, and document upload problems on the MP e-District portal.

How to fix:

  • Use Google Chrome (latest version) and clear cache.
  • Upload documents in JPEG/PDF under 200KB.
  • Try during off-peak hours (early morning).
  • Visit the nearest Lok Seva Kendra / MP Online Kiosk for assisted filing.

Errors in the issued certificate — misspelled names, wrong caste name, or incorrect details.

How to fix:

  • Apply for certificate correction at the Tehsil office with supporting documents.
  • Submit Aadhaar, school records, Samagra ID, and family certificates as evidence.
  • For online certificates, request re-issuance via e-District Portal.
  • If refused, escalate to the District Collector's office.

Applications rejected without providing a written explanation.

How to fix:

  • Request a written reason for rejection from the Tehsildar.
  • File an appeal with the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) within 30 days.
  • Lodge a grievance at Samadhan Portal or call CM Helpline 181.
  • Seek free legal aid from DLSA — NALSA Portal.

MP Online kiosk operators sometimes charge ₹200-₹500 extra beyond the prescribed government fee.

How to fix:

  • Check the official fee on MP e-District Portal before visiting a kiosk.
  • Apply directly online to avoid kiosk charges.
  • Report overcharging to the District Collector or Samadhan Portal.
  • Always collect a printed receipt showing the exact fee charged.
